meta.name: Bearing Model Registry
meta.notes: Generated from database. 41 active models — 31 chat + 10 embedding. v0.9.0 introduces model_class ('chat' | 'embedding') and adds `embedding` as the 13th canonical task type. Embedding tasks route only to embedding models (and vice versa) via a hard class filter in scoring. Embedding seed: openai-embed-3-large/small, voyage-3-large/lite, cohere-embed-v4, mistral-embed-2, greenpt-green-embedding (Qwen3-Embedding-4B on renewable infrastructure), and three open-weight local options — bge-m3, nomic-embed-v2-moe, gte-qwen2-7b. Initial embedding task_fitness is hand-curated from MTEB averages; phase 4 (scripts/ingest-mteb.ts) will replace with benchmark-grounded values via the same provenance flow as LMArena / LiveBench / AA. Pricing invariant: every embedding model has output_per_1m === 0 (embedding APIs bill input tokens only).

scoring_methodology.description: Each model receives per-factor scores from 0.0 to 1.0 across seven dimensions. Combined using user-defined priority weights to produce a ranked recommendation list.

factors.cost: Inverse-normalised against cheapest model. Lower cost = higher score.
factors.speed: Based on latency tiers and inference benchmarks. 1.0 = sub-second.
factors.quality: Curated per-task fitness, optionally blended with normalised external benchmark scores via BENCHMARK_BLEND env var (0..1, default 0).
factors.privacy: Based on data retention policies. 1.0 = no retention, no training on inputs.
factors.transparency: Composite of open_weights, open_training_data, open_methodology, licence_openness, provider_disclosure.
factors.capability: Binary pass/fail for hard requirements. Graduated for soft matches.

benchmarks.description: External benchmark scores are stored in benchmark_snapshots and mapped to bearing slugs via benchmark_aliases. Each source category maps to one or more bearing task types; per-task scores are averaged across all contributing categories.

lmarena.metric: Bradley-Terry rating, normalised linearly within each (category, snapshot_date) cohort.

mteb.citation: Muennighoff, N., Tazi, N., Magne, L., & Reimers, N. (2023). MTEB: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark. arXiv:2210.07316.
mteb.metric: MTEB Overall average, normalised linearly within the embedding-model cohort (min-max). Only 'overall' is currently populated; sub-category buckets are reserved for future scoring if weighting by task sub-type becomes useful.

transparency_methodology.description: Transparency scored across five dimensions, each 0.0-1.0. Composite = mean of all five.
transparency_methodology.fmti_reference: Stanford CRFM FMTI 2025. 100 indicators, 13 companies. Average: 40.69/100. IBM: 95, AI21 Labs: 72, Writer: 67, Anthropic: 37, Google: 36, Amazon: 36, OpenAI: 35, DeepSeek: 30, Meta: 30, Alibaba: 30, Mistral: 15, xAI: 14, Midjourney: 14.
transparency_methodology.fmti_criticism: EleutherAI argues FMTI measures commercial documentation rather than genuine openness, biased against open-source projects. Bearing weights actual accessibility alongside documentation.

sustainability_methodology.description: Three sub-dimensions, each 0.0-1.0. Composite = mean of available (nulls excluded).

dimensions.inference_energy: 1.0=published per-query metrics; 0.8=very small/efficient; 0.6=MoE; 0.4=standard large; null=no data
dimensions.provider_infrastructure: 1.0=100% renewable+heat recovery; 0.8=100% renewable; 0.6=significant commitment+reporting; 0.4=some renewable; 0.2=none; null=no data

bge-m3.name: BAAI BGE-M3
strengths: Dense + sparse + multi-vector in one model
Small enough to run on CPU or 2 GB VRAM

transparency.notes: Open weights with permissive licence; methodology partially documented.

sustainability.notes: Small enough to run on local hardware — user controls the energy source.

transparency.notes: Anthropic FMTI 37. No transparency report submitted. Publishes model cards. No training data disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Smaller model = more efficient inference. Anthropic committed to covering 100% grid upgrade costs, water-efficient cooling, energy price offset for ratepayers. No renewable energy target published.

transparency.notes: Anthropic FMTI 37. No transparency report submitted. Publishes model cards. No training data disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Anthropic committed to covering 100% grid upgrade costs, water-efficient cooling, energy price offset for ratepayers. No renewable energy target published.

claude-opus-4.7.name: Claude Opus 4.7
strengths: Structured output and extended thinking support
weaknesses: High output pricing ($25/M tokens) increases per-task cost
Slower inference due to large model size
Extended thinking adds latency for reasoning tasks

transparency.notes: Closed-source model with limited public disclosure on training data and methodology. Anthropic publishes some research on Constitutional AI and alignment but does not open-source weights or full training details.

sustainability.notes: Large flagship model with high computational cost per inference. Anthropic's sustainability commitments are not prominently published; infrastructure details unavailable. Assume moderate environmental footprint typical of large closed-source providers.

claude-sonnet-4.6.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
strengths: Best balance of cost/speed/quality at Anthropic
weaknesses: Not the cheapest for simple tasks

transparency.notes: Anthropic FMTI 37. No transparency report submitted. Publishes model cards. No training data disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Anthropic committed to covering 100% grid upgrade costs, water-efficient cooling, energy price offset for ratepayers. No renewable energy target published.

transparency.notes: Open weights. Mistral FMTI 15.

sustainability.notes: EU-based. Mistral partnered with EcoDataCenter Sweden (renewable). Paris facility on low-carbon nuclear grid. Launched sustainability auditing tool.

cohere-embed-v4.name: Cohere embed-v4
strengths: Enterprise privacy options (BYOK, deployment in customer cloud)
weaknesses: Slightly behind Voyage on English MTEB

transparency.notes: Closed weights; some methodology disclosed.

sustainability.notes: No published sustainability data.

deepseek-r1.name: DeepSeek R1
strengths: Excellent reasoning at fraction of frontier pricing

transparency.notes: MIT licence + open weights. But FMTI 30 due to minimal corporate disclosure. Open weights != corporate transparency.

sustainability.notes: Reasoning = more tokens = higher energy per query.

deepseek-r1-0528.name: DeepSeek R1 0528
strengths: Major upgrade over R1 — better instruction following

transparency.notes: MIT licence + open weights. DeepSeek FMTI 30.

sustainability.notes: Reasoning = more tokens = higher energy.

transparency.notes: MIT licence + open weights. DeepSeek FMTI 30.

sustainability.notes: MoE architecture with 93.3% memory reduction. Only 37B active params of 671B total. China grid heavily coal-based, no provider sustainability reporting.

deepseek-v3.2.name: DeepSeek V3.2
weaknesses: API can be slow at peak

transparency.notes: MIT + open weights. DeepSeek FMTI 30.

sustainability.notes: MoE efficient.

deepseek-v4-pro.name: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V4 Pro
strengths: Exceptional 1M-token context window for long documents
Open weights enable full data privacy and self-hosting
Strong multi-turn conversation and reasoning capabilities
Efficient MoE architecture balancing quality and speed
weaknesses: Extremely slow inference speed (34 tok/s) limits real-time applications
Limited transparency on training data composition

transparency.notes: Open-weight model enabling self-hosting and full data control. DeepSeek publishes model weights and some technical details, but training data sources not fully disclosed. MoE architecture and reasoning capabilities documented in research papers.

sustainability.notes: DeepSeek operates in China with limited public disclosure of energy infrastructure. Open-weight design allows efficient self-hosted deployment. No official sustainability commitments published. MoE architecture with 49B activated parameters suggests moderate inference efficiency compared to dense alternatives.

devstral.name: Devstral
strengths: Excellent for tool use and code agents

transparency.notes: Apache 2.0 + open weights. Mistral FMTI 15.

sustainability.notes: Small 24B model = efficient inference. Mistral partnered with EcoDataCenter Sweden (renewable). Paris facility on low-carbon nuclear grid. Launched sustainability auditing tool.

gemini-2.5-flash-lite.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
strengths: Cheapest mainstream model from major provider

transparency.notes: Lightest Google model.

sustainability.notes: Lightest Google model. Designed for efficiency on TPU. Google 100% renewable match since 2017. Targeting 24/7 carbon-free by 2030.

gemini-3-flash.name: Gemini 3 Flash
weaknesses: Not quite Pro quality on hardest reasoning

transparency.notes: Google FMTI 36. Dropped 6 points from 2024. Model cards but limited training data disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Flash models designed for efficiency. TPU infrastructure. Google 100% renewable match since 2017. Published per-query energy: 0.24 Wh median text prompt. Targeting 24/7 carbon-free by 2030 (this has been downgraded on new information about Google using gas powered stations for data centres and moving off 2030 target)

transparency.notes: Google FMTI 36. Dropped 6 points from 2024. Model cards but limited training data disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Custom TPUs. Google 100% renewable match since 2017. Published per-query energy: 0.24 Wh median text prompt. Targeting 24/7 carbon-free by 2030. Best mainstream sustainability disclosure.

transparency.notes: OpenAI FMTI 35. Publishes system cards. No training data disclosure. Score dropped from 2024.

sustainability.notes: CEO stated 0.34 Wh per average ChatGPT query. Claims 100% renewable by 2027 via Microsoft Azure RECs (not direct PPAs). Zero published Scope 1/2/3 emissions as of 2026.

gpt-5.4-mini.name: GPT-5.4 Mini
weaknesses: Not as strong on complex reasoning as full 5.4

transparency.notes: OpenAI FMTI 35. Publishes system cards. No training data disclosure. Score dropped from 2024.

sustainability.notes: Smaller model = more efficient. CEO stated 0.34 Wh per average ChatGPT query. Claims 100% renewable by 2027 via Microsoft Azure RECs (not direct PPAs). Zero published Scope 1/2/3 emissions as of 2026.

gpt-5.4-nano.name: GPT-5.4 Nano
weaknesses: Not for complex or creative tasks

transparency.notes: Smallest OpenAI model. Less documented than flagship.

sustainability.notes: Smallest OpenAI model = most efficient inference. CEO stated 0.34 Wh per average ChatGPT query. Claims 100% renewable by 2027 via Microsoft Azure RECs (not direct PPAs). Zero published Scope 1/2/3 emissions as of 2026.

greenpt-green-embedding.name: GreenPT green-embedding (Qwen3-Embedding-4B)
strengths: Heat recovery — server heat heats buildings
Qwen3-Embedding-4B backbone (open model under green infrastructure)
weaknesses: Single provider — smaller community than Cohere / OpenAI

transparency.notes: Open-source backbone; hosting infrastructure documented.

sustainability.notes: 100% renewable energy + heat recovery. Best-in-class for hosted embedding.

greenpt-greenl.name: GreenPT GreenL (Mistral Small 3.2 24B)
strengths: Heat recovery — server heat heats buildings
OCR and Scraper APIs for document processing

transparency.notes: Not scored by FMTI (too small). Publishes real-time energy metrics. Fully open source models. Transparent about limitations.

sustainability.notes: 100% renewable inference. Heat recovery for community heating. Published mWh/100 tokens. PUE 1.37 (industry avg 1.57). WUE 0.067 (industry avg 1.8). 20-30% compute reduction through compression. Training not on green infra (acknowledged).

greenpt-greenr.name: GreenPT GreenR (GPT-OSS 120B)
strengths: Larger model — better reasoning than GreenL

transparency.notes: Same transparency profile as GreenL.

sustainability.notes: Same green infra as GreenL. PUE 1.37 (industry avg 1.57). WUE 0.067 (industry avg 1.8). 20-30% compute reduction through compression. Larger model = slightly higher per-query energy. 120B training footprint larger.

transparency.notes: xAI FMTI 14 — lowest tier. Closed weights. Minimal disclosure.

sustainability.notes: Memphis Colossus running on gas turbines (no emission permits as of 2025). 2GW capacity planned. 88-acre solar array planned but not built. 168 Tesla Megapacks for battery storage. Among worst sustainability profiles.

gte-qwen2-7b.name: Alibaba gte-Qwen2-7B-instruct
strengths: Top-tier open MTEB performance — competitive with hosted flagships
weaknesses: Heavy — 7B params, 8 GB VRAM at Q8

transparency.notes: Open weights with permissive licence; methodology partially documented.

sustainability.notes: Small enough to run on local hardware — user controls the energy source.

ibm-granite-3.3.name: IBM Granite 4.0 Micro
strengths: Part of Granite family (vision, code, guardian variants available)
MATH500 competitive with 24B models despite 8B size
weaknesses: No vision in base (separate Granite Vision models available)

transparency.notes: FMTI 95/100 — highest ever. Perfect 100 on 10/14 dimensions. Full training data documentation. Apache 2.0.

sustainability.notes: Published training compute/energy data. Smaller models run on consumer hardware. FMTI 95 includes disclosure on compute infrastructure.

kimi-k2.name: Kimi K2
strengths: Very competitive pricing for flagship quality

transparency.notes: Open weights with licence restrictions. Not scored by FMTI.

sustainability.notes: MoE efficient at inference. No provider energy data.

transparency.notes: Not FMTI-scored.

sustainability.notes: No provider energy data.

transparency.notes: Open weights with registration. Meta Community Licence restricts competitors. FMTI 30 — open model but opaque company.

sustainability.notes: Meta claims 100% renewable operations (bucket 0.8). Net-zero by 2030. Water-positive by 2030. Dry cooling at new facilities. MoE 17B active = efficient inference.

minimax-m2.5.name: MiniMax M2.5
strengths: Optimized for code generation and refactoring

transparency.notes: Open weights. Not FMTI-scored.

sustainability.notes: Linear attention efficient. No provider data.

transparency.notes: Open weights. Not FMTI-scored.

sustainability.notes: Larger model. No provider energy data.

transparency.notes: Closed weights; some methodology disclosed.

sustainability.notes: No published sustainability data.

transparency.notes: Apache 2.0 + open weights. But FMTI 15 — dropped 37 pts from 2024. Open weights != corporate transparency.

sustainability.notes: EU-based. Mistral partnered with EcoDataCenter Sweden (renewable). Paris facility on low-carbon nuclear grid. Launched sustainability auditing tool.

mistral-ocr.name: Mistral OCR (Pixtral Large)
weaknesses: Specialist model — not for general chat
Higher cost than general models for non-vision tasks

transparency.notes: Open weights with registration. FMTI 15.

sustainability.notes: EU-based. Mistral partnered with EcoDataCenter Sweden (renewable). Paris facility on low-carbon nuclear grid. Launched sustainability auditing tool.

nomic-embed-v2-moe.name: Nomic nomic-embed-text-v2-moe
strengths: Top open English MTEB scores at the 1B-param scale

transparency.notes: Open weights with permissive licence; methodology partially documented.

sustainability.notes: Small enough to run on local hardware — user controls the energy source.

openai-embed-3-large.name: OpenAI text-embedding-3-large
strengths: Matryoshka — dim can be truncated to 256/512/1024/3072

transparency.notes: Closed weights; some methodology disclosed.

sustainability.notes: No published sustainability data.

transparency.notes: Closed weights; some methodology disclosed.

sustainability.notes: No published sustainability data.

transparency.notes: Alibaba FMTI 30 (first year). Open weights with commercial restrictions.

sustainability.notes: Depends on hosting infrastructure.

weaknesses: No vision (use Qwen3-VL variant for vision)

transparency.notes: Apache 2.0, open weights. Alibaba FMTI 30.

sustainability.notes: MoE efficient. Depends on hosting.

qwen3.5-397b.name: Qwen 3.5 397B
